summary: Brand surfaces are visually coherent (warm parchment palette, DM Sans + Source Serif 4, teal-gold gradient mark) but the social layer has two trust gaps: (1) bios lead with product description, not credential signal ΓÇö a nurse scrolling X or Instagram has no reason to believe ChapAI questions are exam-grade; (2) the upgrade page separates CCRN and NCLEX visually but both tracks share identical price IDs and badge language, so the distinctiveness that justifies two separate packages isn't felt. The logo mark is strong; the wordmark subtitle 'CCRN + NCLEX' is accurate but doesn't reinforce trust. Zero social proof, no author/clinician signal anywhere visible.
recommendations: {"id":"R1","title":"Add a single trust line to both bios","effort":"10 min","file":"drafts/social-bios-and-launch-copy.md","change":"Prepend one credential-anchored line to each bio. X: \u0027Nurse-reviewed. Original questions only.\u0027 Instagram: \u0027Nurse-reviewed questions. No recycled banks.\u0027 This is the fastest trust signal that doesn\u0027t require design ΓÇö it works in text before any visual loads.","why":"Bios currently lead with feature list. Exam-prep buyers need to know someone credentialed stands behind the questions before they click a link."}

{"id":"R2","title":"Create two separate launch header visuals ΓÇö one per exam track","effort":"45 min","file":"apps/web/public/brand/ (new files: ccrn-launch-card.svg, nclex-launch-card.svg)","change":"Clone chapai-wordmark.svg. For CCRN variant: swap subtitle text to \u0027CCRN Critical Care Review\u0027 and tint the C-curve stroke to #5A7F88 (teal only). For NCLEX variant: subtitle \u0027NCLEX Licensure Prep\u0027, tint C-curve to #C29A56 (gold only). Keep everything else identical. Use as pinned post header images and link preview og:image per exam subdomain.","why":"The wordmark currently says \u0027CCRN + NCLEX\u0027 ΓÇö when used as the pinned post image for either exam account it signals a generic product, not a dedicated one. Separate cards cost one SVG duplication and two text edits."}

{"id":"R3","title":"Add exam-specific trust badge line above the plan h1 on upgrade page","effort":"15 min","file":"apps/web/src/app/upgrade/page.tsx","change":"Below the existing \u0027Plans\u0027 span and above the h1, add: \u003cp className=\u0027mt-2 text-sm text-muted\u0027\u003eOriginal questions. Nurse-reviewed. No recycled question banks.\u003c/p\u003e ΓÇö one static line, no data dependency. This is the last thing a user reads before seeing a price.","why":"The upgrade page has zero trust copy between the header and price cards. A $29ΓÇô$59/mo ask from an unknown brand needs a belief statement at the decision point."}

{"id":"R4","title":"Differentiate pinned post copy by exam track ΓÇö do not cross-post identical text","effort":"20 min","file":"drafts/social-bios-and-launch-copy.md","change":"Add two distinct pinned post variants. CCRN pinned: lead with \u0027Built for working ICU nurses studying for CCRN.\u0027 NCLEX pinned: lead with \u0027Built for new grads who need to pass NCLEX on the first attempt.\u0027 Keep bullets identical below the lead. The audience emotional hook is completely different and the current copy serves neither well.","why":"The current pinned post is exam-agnostic. CCRN buyers are experienced nurses; NCLEX buyers are new grads. Same copy for both = low relevance = low click intent."}

skills: SVG variant generation from existing brand files by text/stroke-color substitution ΓÇö no Figma needed
Upgrade page trust gap: zero social proof between header and pricing; one static line fixes it fast
durable_memory: ChapAI brand palette: bg #F5F1E8, dark #1F262B, muted #657278, teal #5A7F88, gold #C29A56
ChapAI fonts: DM Sans (UI/headings), Source Serif 4 (body/serif)
CCRN audience = experienced ICU nurses; NCLEX audience = new grads ΓÇö copy must split on this axis
Upgrade page price IDs are currently shared between CCRN and NCLEX tracks ΓÇö differentiation is visual only
Social bios have no credential/trust signal; feature-first copy is the current gap
active_context: Task: trust-building brand upgrade for X and Instagram ΓÇö minimal new design
All four recommendations are executable in under 2 hours with no external dependencies
R2 (separate launch cards) is the highest-leverage visual asset; R1 and R4 are copy-only and can ship in minutes

current_blocker: None within the repo. All changes are self-contained SVG edits, TSX edits, and markdown edits. External blocker: no clinician attribution copy exists yet ΓÇö 'Nurse-reviewed' language in R1/R3/R4 requires confirmation that a licensed nurse has actually reviewed the question set before that line is published.
suggested_human_fix: Confirm whether a licensed RN has reviewed the question corpus. If yes, 'Nurse-reviewed' is accurate and should be added immediately to all three surfaces (bio, pinned post, upgrade page). If no, substitute 'Clinically referenced' or 'Built for CCRN exam objectives' until review is complete. This is the only trust claim that could backfire if unverified.
next_task: Execute R2: duplicate chapai-wordmark.svg into ccrn-launch-card.svg and nclex-launch-card.svg, swap subtitle text and single-color stroke tints per exam track, export as 1200x420 PNG for og:image and pinned post header use.
